596: /**
597:  * Extracts a display title from indexed content.
598:  * @param content - The content to inspect.
599:  * @param filename - The optional source filename.
600:  * @returns The extracted title.
601:  */
602: export function extract_title(content: unknown, filename?: string): string {
603:   if (!content || typeof content !== 'string') {
604:     return filename ? path.basename(filename, path.extname(filename)) : 'Untitled';
605:   }
606: 
607:   const h1_match = content.match(/^#\s+(.+)$/m);
608:   if (h1_match && h1_match[1]) {
609:     return h1_match[1].trim();
610:   }
611: 
612:   const h2_match = content.match(/^##\s+(.+)$/m);
613:   if (h2_match && h2_match[1]) {
614:     return h2_match[1].trim();
615:   }
616: 
617:   const yaml_match = content.match(/^---[\s\S]*?^title:\s*(.+)$/m);
618:   if (yaml_match && yaml_match[1]) {
619:     return yaml_match[1].trim().replace(/^["']|["']$/g, '');
620:   }
621: 
622:   const lines = content.split('\n').filter(l => l.trim().length > 0);
623:   if (lines.length > 0) {
624:     const first_line = lines[0].trim();
625:     return first_line.replace(/^#+\s*/, '').substring(0, 100);
626:   }
627: 
628:   return filename ? path.basename(filename, path.extname(filename)) : 'Untitled';
629: }
630: 
631: /**
632:  * Extracts a preview snippet from indexed content.
633:  * @param content - The content to inspect.
634:  * @param max_length - The maximum snippet length.
635:  * @returns The extracted snippet.
636:  */
637: export function extract_snippet(content: unknown, max_length = 200): string {
638:   if (!content || typeof content !== 'string') {
639:     return '';
640:   }
641: 
642:   const text = content.replace(/^---[\s\S]*?---\n*/m, '');
643:   const lines = text.split('\n');
644:   const snippet_lines: string[] = [];
645: 
646:   for (const line of lines) {
647:     const trimmed = line.trim();
648: 
649:     if (!trimmed || /^#+\s/.test(trimmed)) {
650:       if (snippet_lines.length > 0) {
651:         break;
652:       }
653:       continue;
654:     }
655: 
656:     if (/^[a-z_-]+:\s/i.test(trimmed) && snippet_lines.length === 0) {
657:       continue;
658:     }
659: 
660:     snippet_lines.push(trimmed);
661: 
662:     const current_length = snippet_lines.join(' ').length;
663:     if (current_length >= max_length) {
664:       break;
665:     }
666:   }
667: 
668:   let snippet = snippet_lines.join(' ');
669: 
670:   if (snippet.length > max_length) {
671:     snippet = snippet.substring(0, max_length);
672:     const last_space = snippet.lastIndexOf(' ');
673:     if (last_space > max_length * 0.7) {
674:       snippet = snippet.substring(0, last_space);
675:     }
676:     snippet += '...';
677:   }
678: 
679:   return snippet;
680: }
681: 
682: /**
683:  * Extracts tags from indexed content.
684:  * @param content - The content to inspect.
685:  * @returns The extracted tags.
686:  */
687: export function extract_tags(content: unknown): string[] {
688:   if (!content || typeof content !== 'string') {
689:     console.warn('[vector-index] extract_tags: invalid content type, expected string');
690:     return [];
691:   }
692: 
693:   const tags = new Set<string>();
694: 
695:   const yaml_tags_match = content.match(/^---[\s\S]*?^tags:\s*\[([^\]]+)\]/m);
696:   if (yaml_tags_match && yaml_tags_match[1]) {
697:     yaml_tags_match[1].split(',').forEach(tag => {
698:       const cleaned = tag.trim().replace(/^["']|["']$/g, '');
699:       if (cleaned) tags.add(cleaned.toLowerCase());
700:     });
701:   }
702: 
703:   const yaml_list_match = content.match(/^---[\s\S]*?^tags:\s*\n((?:\s*-\s*.+\n?)+)/m);
704:   if (yaml_list_match && yaml_list_match[1]) {
705:     yaml_list_match[1].match(/-\s*(.+)/g)?.forEach(match => {
706:       const tag = match.replace(/^-\s*/, '').trim().replace(/^["']|["']$/g, '');
707:       if (tag) tags.add(tag.toLowerCase());
708:     });
709:   }
710: 
711:   const hashtag_matches = content.match(/(?:^|\s)#([a-zA-Z][a-zA-Z0-9_-]*)/g);
712:   if (hashtag_matches) {
713:     hashtag_matches.forEach(match => {
714:       const tag = match.trim().replace(/^#/, '');
715:       if (tag && !tag.match(/^[0-9]+$/)) {
716:         tags.add(tag.toLowerCase());
717:       }
718:     });
719:   }
720: 
721:   return Array.from(tags);
722: }
723: 
724: /**
725:  * Extracts a relevant date from indexed content or path.
726:  * @param content - The content to inspect.
727:  * @param file_path - The optional source file path.
728:  * @returns The extracted date, if available.
729:  */
730: export function extract_date(content: unknown, file_path?: string): string | null {
731:   if (content && typeof content === 'string') {
732:     const date_match = content.match(/^---[\s\S]*?^date:\s*(.+)$/m);
733:     if (date_match && date_match[1]) {
734:       const date_str = date_match[1].trim().replace(/^["']|["']$/g, '');
735:       try {
736:         const parsed = new Date(date_str);
737:         if (!isNaN(parsed.getTime())) {
738:           return parsed.toISOString().split('T')[0];
739:         }
740:       } catch (_e: unknown) {
741:         console.warn('[vector-index-queries] Date parsing failed', { error: _e instanceof Error ? _e.message : String(_e) });
742:       }
743:     }
744:   }
745: 
746:   if (file_path) {
747:     const filename = path.basename(file_path);
748: 
749:     const iso_match = filename.match(/(\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2})/);
750:     if (iso_match) {
751:       return iso_match[1];
752:     }
753: 
754:     const ddmmyy_match = filename.match(/(\d{2})-(\d{2})-(\d{2})/);
755:     if (ddmmyy_match) {
756:       const [, day, month, year] = ddmmyy_match;
757:       const full_year = parseInt(year) > 50 ? `19${year}` : `20${year}`;
758:       return `${full_year}-${month}-${day}`;
759:     }
760:   }
761: 
762:   return null;
763: }

765: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
766:    EMBEDDING GENERATION WRAPPER
767: ----------------------------------------------------------------*/

769: /**
770:  * Generates an embedding for a search query.
771:  * @param query - The search query.
772:  * @returns A promise that resolves to the embedding, if generated.
773:  */
774: export async function generate_query_embedding(query: string): Promise<Float32Array | null> {
775:   if (!query || typeof query !== 'string' || query.trim().length === 0) {
776:     console.warn('[vector-index] Empty query provided for embedding');
777:     return null;
778:   }
779: 
780:   try {
781:     const database = initialize_db();
782:     const active = getActiveEmbedder(database);
783:     const trimmedQuery = query.trim();
784:     const modelId = active.name !== DEFAULT_ACTIVE_EMBEDDER.name
785:       ? active.name
786:       : embeddingsProvider.getModelName();
787:     const embeddingDim = active.name !== DEFAULT_ACTIVE_EMBEDDER.name
788:       ? active.dim
789:       : embeddingsProvider.getEmbeddingDimension();
790:     const profileKey = getActiveEmbeddingProfileKey(database, modelId, embeddingDim);
791:     const contentHash = computeContentHash(trimmedQuery);
792:     const cached = lookupEmbedding(database, contentHash, modelId, embeddingDim, {
793:       profileKey,
794:       inputKind: 'query',
795:     });
796:     if (cached) {
797:       return new Float32Array(
798:         cached.buffer,
799:         cached.byteOffset,
800:         Math.floor(cached.byteLength / Float32Array.BYTES_PER_ELEMENT),
801:       );
802:     }
803: 
804:     const provider = active.name !== DEFAULT_ACTIVE_EMBEDDER.name
805:       ? active.provider ?? getManifest(active.name)?.backend ?? 'ollama'
806:       : getStartupEmbeddingProfile().provider;
807:     const adapter = getEmbedderAdapter(provider, modelId, embeddingDim);
808:     const activeAdapter = adapter as typeof adapter & {
809:       embed: (
810:         texts: ReadonlyArray<string>,
811:         options?: { inputType?: 'query' | 'document' },
812:       ) => Promise<Float32Array[]>;
813:     };
814:     const [activeEmbedding] = await activeAdapter.embed([trimmedQuery], { inputType: 'query' });
815:     const embedding: Float32Array | null = activeEmbedding ?? null;
816: 
817:     if (embedding) {
818:       storeEmbedding(
819:         database,
820:         contentHash,
821:         modelId,
822:         Buffer.from(embedding.buffer, embedding.byteOffset, embedding.byteLength),
823:         embedding.length,
824:         { profileKey, inputKind: 'query' },
825:       );
826:     }
827: 
828:     return embedding;
829:   } catch (error: unknown) {
830:     console.warn(`[vector-index] Query embedding failed: ${get_error_message(error)}`);
831:     return null;
832:   }
833: }

839: /**
840:  * Searches indexed memories using keyword matching.
841:  * @param query - The search query.
842:  * @param options - Search options.
843:  * @returns The matching memory rows.
844:  */
845: export function keyword_search(
846:   query: string,
847:   options: { limit?: number; specFolder?: string | null } = {},
848:   database: Database.Database = initialize_db(),
849: ): MemoryRow[] {
850:   const { limit = 20, specFolder = null } = options;
851: 
852:   if (!query || typeof query !== 'string') {
853:     console.warn('[vector-index] keyword_search: invalid query, expected non-empty string');
854:     return [];
855:   }
856: 
857:   const search_terms = query.toLowerCase().trim().split(/\s+/).filter(t => t.length >= 2);
858:   if (search_terms.length === 0) {
859:     console.warn('[vector-index] keyword_search: no valid search terms after tokenization');
860:     return [];
861:   }
862: 
863:   let where_clause = '1=1';
864:   const params: unknown[] = [];
865: 
866:   if (specFolder) {
867:     where_clause += " AND (spec_folder = ? OR spec_folder LIKE ? ESCAPE '\\')";
868:     params.push(specFolder, specFolderLikePattern(specFolder));
869:   }
870: 
871:   const sql = `
872:     SELECT m.* FROM memory_index m
873:     JOIN active_memory_projection p ON p.active_memory_id = m.id
874:     WHERE ${where_clause}
875:     ORDER BY m.importance_weight DESC, m.created_at DESC
876:   `;
877: 
878:   const rows = database.prepare(sql).all(...params);
879: 
880:   const scored = (rows as MemoryRow[]).map((row: MemoryRow) => {
881:     let score = 0;
882:     const searchable_text = [
883:       row.title || '',
884:       parse_trigger_phrases(row.trigger_phrases).join(' '),
885:       row.spec_folder || '',
886:       row.file_path || ''
887:     ].join(' ').toLowerCase();
888: 
889:     for (const term of search_terms) {
890:       if (searchable_text.includes(term)) {
891:         score += 1;
892:         if ((row.title || '').toLowerCase().includes(term)) {
893:           score += 2;
894:         }
895:         if (parse_trigger_phrases(row.trigger_phrases).join(' ').toLowerCase().includes(term)) {
896:           score += 1.5;
897:         }
898:       }
899:     }
900: 
901:     score *= (0.5 + (row.importance_weight ?? 0));
902:     return { ...row, keyword_score: score };
903:   });
904: 
905:   const filtered = scored
906:     .filter((row: MemoryRow) => Number(row.keyword_score ?? 0) > 0)
907:     .sort((a: MemoryRow, b: MemoryRow) => Number(b.keyword_score ?? 0) - Number(a.keyword_score ?? 0))
908:     .slice(0, limit);
909: 
910:   return filtered.map((row: MemoryRow) => {
911:     row.trigger_phrases = parse_trigger_phrases(row.trigger_phrases);
912:     row.isConstitutional = row.importance_tier === 'constitutional';
913:     return row;
914:   });
915: }
916: 
917: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
918:    ENRICHED VECTOR SEARCH
919: ----------------------------------------------------------------*/
920: 
921: /**
922:  * Runs enriched vector search for a text query.
923:  * @param query - The search query.
924:  * @param limit - The maximum number of results to return.
925:  * @param options - Search options.
926:  * @returns A promise that resolves to enriched search results.
927:  * @throws {VectorIndexError} Propagates vector-store initialization failures from the underlying search pipeline.
928:  * @example
929:  * ```ts
930:  * const results = await vector_search_enriched('sqlite vec mismatch', 10, { specFolder: 'specs/001-demo' });
931:  * ```
932:  */
933: export async function vector_search_enriched(
934:   query: string,
935:   limit = 20,
936:   options: { specFolder?: string | null; minSimilarity?: number } = {},
937:   database: Database.Database = initialize_db(),
938: ): Promise<EnrichedSearchResult[]> {
939:   const start_time = Date.now();
940:   const { specFolder = null, minSimilarity = 30 } = options;
941: 
942:   const query_embedding = await generate_query_embedding(query);
943: 
944:   let raw_results;
945:   let search_method = 'vector';
946: 
947:   const sqlite_vec = get_sqlite_vec_available();
948:   if (query_embedding && sqlite_vec) {
949:     raw_results = vector_search(query_embedding, {
950:       limit,
951:       specFolder,
952:       minSimilarity
953:     }, database);
954:   } else {
955:     console.warn('[vector-index] Falling back to keyword search');
956:     search_method = 'keyword';
957:     raw_results = keyword_search(query, { limit, specFolder }, database);
958:   }
959: 
960:   // HIGH-004 FIX: Read all files concurrently
961:   const file_contents = await Promise.all(

963:   );
964: 
965:   const enriched_results = raw_results.map((row: MemoryRow, i: number) => {

967:     const title = row.title || extract_title(content, row.file_path);
968:     const snippet = extract_snippet(content);
969:     const tags = extract_tags(content);
970:     const date = extract_date(content, row.file_path) || row.created_at?.split('T')[0] || null;
971: 
972:     const similarity = search_method === 'vector'
973:       ? (row.similarity || 0)
974:       : Math.min(100, (row.keyword_score || 0) * 20);
975: 
976:     return {
977:       rank: i + 1,
978:       similarity: Math.round(similarity * 100) / 100,
979:       title,
980:       specFolder: row.spec_folder,
981:       filePath: row.file_path,
982:       date,
983:       tags,
984:       snippet,

989:     };
990:   });

231:  * guarantee is enforced via Stage4ReadonlyRow readonly fields; the runtime check
232:  * via captureScoreSnapshot / verifyScoreInvariant provides defence-in-depth. If
233:  * verifyScoreInvariant throws, it indicates a Stage 4 implementation bug.
234:  *

236:  * It happens after the cache layer in the main handler to avoid cache pollution.

243:   const stageStart = Date.now();

245:

249:   // It is taken over the FULL input set BEFORE any operations, so rows that
250:   // Are subsequently filtered out are still in the before-snapshot. The
251:   // VerifyScoreInvariant call skips rows not present in the after-set.
252:   const scoresBefore = captureScoreSnapshot(results);
253:

255:   const filterResult = filterByMemoryState(
256:     results,
257:     config.minState,
258:     config.applyStateLimits,
259:   );
260: 
261:   let workingResults: Stage4ReadonlyRow[] = filterResult.filtered;
262:   const stateFiltered = filterResult.removedCount;
263:

265:   let evidenceGapDetected = false;
266:   let evidenceGapWarning: string | undefined;
267: 
268:   if (isTRMEnabled()) {

274:     if (trm.gapDetected) {

277:       // Annotate each result with the gap metadata (non-score field).
278:       workingResults = workingResults.map(row => ({

280:         evidenceGap: { gapDetected: true, warning: evidenceGapWarning },
281:       }));
282:     }
283:   }
284:

295:     after: filterResult.statsAfter,

297:   };

303:   };

241:     ...candidate,
242:     ...existing,
243:     content: firstString(existing.content, candidate.content, candidate.content_text),
244:     content_text: firstString(existing.content_text, candidate.content_text, candidate.content),
245:     precomputedContent: firstString(existing.precomputedContent, candidate.precomputedContent),
246:     trigger_phrases: firstString(existing.trigger_phrases, candidate.trigger_phrases),
247:     triggerPhrases: firstString(existing.triggerPhrases, candidate.triggerPhrases),
248:     document_type: firstString(existing.document_type, candidate.document_type),
249:     spec_folder: firstString(existing.spec_folder, candidate.spec_folder),
250:     file_path: firstString(existing.file_path, candidate.file_path),
251:     title: firstString(existing.title, candidate.title),
252:   };
253: }
254: 
255: function syncScore(row: PipelineRow, score: number): ScoredRow {
256:   return {
257:     ...row,
258:     score,
259:     rrfScore: score,
260:     intentAdjustedScore: score,
261:   };
262: }

264: function fetchSiblingRows(db: Database.Database, rows: PipelineRow[], maxPerFolder: number): PipelineRow[] {
265:   const folders = Array.from(new Set(
266:     rows
267:       .map((row) => row.spec_folder)
268:       .filter((folder): folder is string => typeof folder === 'string' && folder.length > 0)
269:   ));
270:   if (folders.length === 0) return [];
271: 
272:   const siblings: PipelineRow[] = [];
273:   const stmt = db.prepare(`
274:     SELECT *
275:     FROM memory_index
276:     WHERE spec_folder = ?
277:       AND document_type IN (${SIBLING_DOCUMENT_TYPES.map(() => '?').join(',')})
278:     ORDER BY
279:       CASE document_type
280:         WHEN 'spec' THEN 0
281:         WHEN 'decision_record' THEN 1
282:         WHEN 'implementation_summary' THEN 2
283:         WHEN 'tasks' THEN 3
284:         WHEN 'checklist' THEN 4
285:         WHEN 'plan' THEN 5
286:         ELSE 6
287:       END,
288:       id ASC
289:     LIMIT ?
290:   `);
291: 
292:   for (const folder of folders) {
293:     const rowsForFolder = stmt.all(folder, ...SIBLING_DOCUMENT_TYPES, maxPerFolder) as PipelineRow[];
294:     siblings.push(...rowsForFolder);
295:   }
296:   return siblings;
297: }

299: function fetchLexicalBackfillRows(db: Database.Database, query: string): PipelineRow[] {
300:   const tokens = importantTokens(queryTokens(query)).slice(0, 10);
301:   if (tokens.length === 0) return [];
302: 
303:   const clauses = tokens.map(() => `
304:     LOWER(COALESCE(title, '') || ' ' || COALESCE(trigger_phrases, '') || ' ' || COALESCE(file_path, '') || ' ' || COALESCE(content_text, '')) LIKE ?
305:   `);
306:   const params = tokens.map((token) => `%${token}%`);
307: 
308:   try {
309:     return db.prepare(`
310:       SELECT *, content_text AS content
311:       FROM memory_index
312:       WHERE ${clauses.join(' OR ')}
313:       ORDER BY
314:         CASE document_type
315:           WHEN 'decision_record' THEN 0
316:           WHEN 'implementation_summary' THEN 1
317:           WHEN 'tasks' THEN 2
318:           WHEN 'spec' THEN 3
319:           WHEN 'checklist' THEN 4
320:           WHEN 'plan' THEN 5
321:           ELSE 6
322:         END,
323:         quality_score DESC,
324:         id ASC
325:       LIMIT 250
326:     `).all(...params) as PipelineRow[];
327:   } catch {
328:     return [];
329:   }
330: }

332: function hydrateCandidateRows(db: Database.Database, rows: PipelineRow[]): PipelineRow[] {
333:   if (rows.length === 0) return rows;
334:   const stmt = db.prepare('SELECT *, content_text AS content FROM memory_index WHERE id = ?');
335:   return rows.map((row) => {
336:     try {
337:       const hydrated = stmt.get(row.id) as PipelineRow | undefined;
338:       return hydrated ? mergeRicherRow(row, hydrated) : row;
339:     } catch {
340:       return row;
341:     }
342:   });
343: }
344: 
345: // Build a boundary gate for NEWLY-injected rescue rows, mirroring the
346: // scope/folder re-application that constitutional injection and the
347: // community fallback perform after their own re-queries. Returns null when
348: // no scope or folder constraint is in force (nothing to enforce).
349: function buildInjectionBoundary(options: RescueOptions): ((row: PipelineRow) => boolean) | null {
350:   const scopeFilter = options.scopeFilter;
351:   const hasScope = Boolean(scopeFilter && (scopeFilter.tenantId || scopeFilter.userId || scopeFilter.agentId));
352:   const scopePredicate = hasScope ? createScopeFilterPredicate<PipelineRow>(scopeFilter as ScopeContext) : null;
353:   const specFolder = options.specFolder;
354:   if (!scopePredicate && !specFolder) return null;
355: 
356:   return (row: PipelineRow): boolean => {
357:     if (scopePredicate && !scopePredicate(row)) return false;
358:     if (specFolder) {
359:       const folder = typeof row.spec_folder === 'string' ? row.spec_folder : '';
360:       // Same prefix rule the BM25 lane uses (hybrid-search.ts): exact folder
361:       // or a descendant path under it.
362:       if (folder !== specFolder && !folder.startsWith(specFolder + '/')) return false;
363:     }
364:     return true;
365:   };
366: }
367: 
368: function mergeSiblingCandidates(query: string, rows: PipelineRow[], options: RescueOptions): PipelineRow[] {
369:   if (!options.db) return rows;
370:   const hydratedRows = hydrateCandidateRows(options.db, rows);
371:   const maxSiblingsPerFolder = options.maxSiblingsPerFolder ?? 8;
372:   const backfill = fetchLexicalBackfillRows(options.db, query);
373:   const siblings = fetchSiblingRows(options.db, [...hydratedRows, ...backfill], maxSiblingsPerFolder);
374:   if (siblings.length === 0 && backfill.length === 0) return hydratedRows;
375: 
376:   const byId = new Map<number, PipelineRow>();
377:   for (const row of hydratedRows) byId.set(row.id, row);
378: 
379:   // Newly-injected backfill/sibling rows bypass the Stage-1 scope gate, so
380:   // re-apply scope/folder boundaries to them only. Existing-id merges already
381:   // passed Stage-1 scoping and are left untouched.
382:   const passesBoundary = buildInjectionBoundary(options);
383: 
384:   for (const candidate of [...backfill, ...siblings]) {
385:     const existing = byId.get(candidate.id);
386:     if (existing) {
387:       byId.set(candidate.id, mergeRicherRow(existing, candidate));
388:       continue;
389:     }
390:     if (passesBoundary && !passesBoundary(candidate)) continue;
391:     const rescue = lexicalScore(query, candidate, options.artifactClass);
392:     if (rescue.score < 0.24) continue;
393:     byId.set(candidate.id, {
394:       ...candidate,
395:       retrievalRescueSibling: true,
396:     });
397:   }
398: 
399:   return Array.from(byId.values());
400: }

402: export function applyRetrievalRescueLayer(
403:   query: string,
404:   rows: PipelineRow[],
405:   options: RescueOptions = {},
406: ): PipelineRow[] {
407:   if (!isRetrievalRescueEnabled() || rows.length === 0) return rows;
408: 
409:   const expanded = mergeSiblingCandidates(query, rows, options);
410:   const rescued = expanded.map((row): ScoredRow => {
411:     const baseScore = resolveEffectiveScore(row);
412:     const rescue = lexicalScore(query, row, options.artifactClass);
413:     // Rescue candidates should compete after the outer normalization
414:     // clamp, not be held below original-lane candidates by a local 0.82 cap.
415:     const score = computeRescueLayerScore(baseScore, rescue.score);
416:     const boost = Math.max(0, score - baseScore);
417:     return {
418:       ...syncScore(row, score),
419:       retrievalRescueScore: rescue.score,
420:       retrievalRescueBoost: boost,
421:       retrievalRescueSignals: rescue.signals,
422:     };
423:   });
424: 
425:   const sorted = rescued.sort((a, b) => {
426:     const scoreDelta = resolveEffectiveScore(b) - resolveEffectiveScore(a);
427:     if (scoreDelta !== 0) return scoreDelta;
428:     const aRescue = a.retrievalRescueScore ?? 0;
429:     const bRescue = b.retrievalRescueScore ?? 0;
430:     if (bRescue !== aRescue) return bRescue - aRescue;
431:     return a.id - b.id;
432:   });
433: 
434:   telemetryCounters.rescueRuns += 1;
435:   const topKHits = sorted
436:     .slice(0, RESCUE_TOP_K)
437:     .filter((row) => (row.retrievalRescueBoost ?? 0) > 0).length;
438:   telemetryCounters.rescueTopKHits += topKHits;
439:   logger.debug('retrieval rescue summary', {
440:     event: 'retrieval_rescue_hit_rate',
441:     topK: RESCUE_TOP_K,
442:     topKHits,
443:     hitRate: topKHits / Math.max(1, Math.min(RESCUE_TOP_K, sorted.length)),
444:     cumulativeRuns: telemetryCounters.rescueRuns,
445:     cumulativeTopKHits: telemetryCounters.rescueTopKHits,
446:   });
447: 
448:   return sorted;
449: }

 *
 * @param row - A Stage4ReadonlyRow to inspect.

/**
 * Extract the best available numeric score from a row for evidence-gap
 * analysis. Delegates to resolveEffectiveScore (canonical chain in types.ts)
 * so scoring, sorting, and filtering all agree on precedence and normalization.
 *
 * Previously used a different precedence order (rrfScore first) and did NOT
 * divide similarity by 100, causing a scale mismatch on rows that only had
 * the similarity field set.
 *
 * @param row - A Stage4ReadonlyRow to inspect.
 * @returns The best available numeric score clamped to [0,1], or 0.
 */
export function extractScoringValue(row: Stage4ReadonlyRow): number {
  return resolveEffectiveScore(row as unknown as PipelineRow);
}

/* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   5. MAIN: executeStage4
   ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */

/**
 * Execute Stage 4 of the 4-stage retrieval pipeline: Filter + Annotate.
 *
 * Stage 4 is the final pipeline stage. It removes results that fall below
 * the configured minimum memory state, runs the Transparent Reasoning Module
 * (TRM) evidence-gap check, attaches annotation metadata, and verifies the
 * score invariant before returning.
 *
 * **Score invariant:** No score fields are modified in Stage 4. The compile-time
 * guarantee is enforced via Stage4ReadonlyRow readonly fields; the runtime check
 * via captureScoreSnapshot / verifyScoreInvariant provides defence-in-depth. If
 * verifyScoreInvariant throws, it indicates a Stage 4 implementation bug.
 *
 * **Session dedup:** Session deduplication is intentionally NOT performed here.
